extractions.problem: Sharing RAN infrastructure among multiple virtual mobile operators (tenants) to reduce CAPEX/OPEX in the face of growing mobile data demand.
extractions.method: Designed a RAN Multi-tenant cell Slicing Controller (RMSC) with three architectures: fully distributed, semi-centralized, and fully centralized.
extractions.finding: Proposed RMSC schemes significantly outperform traditional static slicing and centralized load balancing solutions for multi-tenant mobile networks under uneven user distributions.
extractions.dataset: Simulated multi-tenant mobile network scenarios with uneven user location distributions
extractions.metric: Resource allocation performance vs. baseline solutions
extractions.baseline: Distributed static slicing, centralized load balancing
extractions.result: Significant outperformance of traditional solutions

extractions.problem: Estimating charge separation and recombination rate constants of a bipolar photogalvanic cell through simulation.
extractions.method: Developed a Virtual Basic-based virtual device simulator for bipolar photogalvanic cells; fabricated an actual device using [Ru(bpy)3²⁺] sensitizer and Prussian Blue mediator.
extractions.finding: Charge separation and recombination rate constants were estimated at 5×10² and 6×10⁹ mol⁻¹cm³s⁻¹ respectively; photocurrent response enables estimation of kd and kr.
extractions.dataset: Simulated and fabricated bipolar photogalvanic cell with [Ru(bpy)3²⁺]/Prussian Blue
extractions.metric: Short-circuit photocurrent (JSC), open-circuit photovoltage (VOC), fill-factor
extractions.result: JSC=2.3μA/cm², VOC=0.118V, fill-factor=20.5%

extractions.problem: Reducing power consumption of base stations in wireless networks, which is a significant OPEX concern in the ITC sector.
extractions.method: Implemented a virtual cell concept on base stations and proposed an algorithm for low traffic conditions to improve energy efficiency.
extractions.finding: The virtual cell concept applied to base stations can reduce power consumption under low traffic conditions.
extractions.dataset: Simulated wireless network base stations
extractions.metric: Power consumption

extractions.problem: Understanding how virtual memory T cells contribute to effector and resident memory responses within infected tissues.
extractions.method: Investigated CCR2⁺ and CCR2⁻ virtual memory T cell subsets and their differential roles in immune responses.
extractions.finding: CCR2⁺ and CCR2⁻ virtual memory T cells differentially contribute to effector and resident memory responses within infected tissues.

extractions.problem: Programs often exceed available physical memory, requiring overlay techniques that burden programmers; automating memory management through virtual memory is needed.
extractions.method: Studied replacement algorithms for virtual-storage computer systems that automatically manage memory-overlay functions.
extractions.finding: Virtual memory with automatic replacement algorithms can free programmers from manual overlay management by providing a sufficiently large address range.
extractions.dataset: Virtual-storage computer system simulations

extractions.problem: Efficient allocation of wireless resources to multiple service providers in virtualized multi-cell LTE networks while respecting SLA constraints.
extractions.method: Developed a centralized heuristic for radio resource block allocation in multi-cell LTE; proposed an iterative DC programming solution for non-convex power allocation.
extractions.finding: The heuristic produces results close to the iterative method; SLA contracts cause significant rate reduction; the heuristic meets required rates over multiple scheduling periods even if it cannot in a single period.
extractions.dataset: Simulated multi-cell LTE network with multiple service providers
extractions.metric: Network-wide sum rate, SLA satisfaction
extractions.result: Heuristic close to iterative method results

extractions.problem: Obtaining a comprehensive brain-wide, cellular-level connectivity map (mesoscale connectome) for the mouse, which is fundamental for understanding neural information processing.
extractions.method: Used EGFP-expressing adeno-associated viral vectors for axonal tracing and high-throughput serial two-photon tomography for imaging, registered into a common 3D reference space.
extractions.finding: Created the Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas revealing connectional strength distribution, network symmetry, 3D topography via virtual tractography, and segregation/integration of cortico-thalamic pathways.
extractions.dataset: Mouse brain, Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas
extractions.metric: Connectional strength, network symmetry properties
extractions.baseline: C. elegans microscale connectome (only prior complete dataset)
extractions.result: Whole-brain connectivity matrix in common 3D reference space

extractions.problem: Optimizing performance of dual virtual cell-based systems in cognitive overlay convergent heterogeneous networks with increasing data traffic.
extractions.method: Proposed a Distributed Wireless Communication System with Active and Candidate Virtual Cells using Space-Time Trellis Codes for multi-user high-rate data transmission.
extractions.finding: The dual virtual cell system with cognitive ability and STTC can protect link quality and enable high-rate data transmission; quantitative simulation results confirm system effectiveness under varying parameters.
extractions.dataset: Simulated overlay convergent network with heterogeneous base stations
extractions.metric: System performance under varying environmental parameters

extractions.problem: Identifying novel GSK-3β kinase inhibitors as potential Alzheimer's disease therapeutics that reduce tau aggregation.
extractions.method: Virtual screening of >1.1M compounds (ZINC and in-house databases) using GOLD docking, followed by luminescent kinase assay and cell assays in HEK293 and SH-SY5Y cells.
extractions.finding: Identified VB-003 (IC50=0.25μM) and VB-008/AM404 (IC50=5.4μM) as GSK-3β inhibitors reducing tau aggregation; VB-008 also enhanced HSPB1/GRP78 expression and promoted neurite outgrowth.
extractions.dataset: >1.1M compounds (ZINC + in-house), HEK293 cells, ΔK280 tauRD-DsRed SH-SY5Y cells
extractions.metric: IC50, tau aggregation reduction, phospho-GSK-3β expression

extractions.problem: Conceptualizing virtual manufacturing cells for flexible production systems.
Understanding the protective and pathological roles of virtual memory T (TVM) cells in immune-related diseases, which remain largely unknown.
extractions.method: Review of current literature on TVM cell characteristics, immunological properties, and roles in infections and cancers.
extractions.finding: TVM cells play first-line defense roles similar to innate cells against bacterial and viral infections, with emerging evidence for roles in various immune-related diseases including cancers.

extractions.problem: Reducing CSI overhead and inter-virtual-cell interference in ultra dense networks where overlapping virtual cells form clusters.
extractions.method: Proposed hierarchical beamforming: intra-cluster Zero-Forcing block diagonalization with perfect CSI, inter-cluster iterative power allocation via generalized weighted MMSE using only large-scale CSI.
extractions.finding: The proposed hierarchical scheme achieves significant performance gain over traditional schemes while requiring less channel state information.
extractions.dataset: Simulated ultra dense network with virtual cell clusters
extractions.metric: Achievable rate, CSI overhead
extractions.result: Significant performance gain with less CSI

extractions.problem: Determining whether rodent hippocampal place coding has a homologue in humans or whether human navigation relies on a different, visually-based mechanism.
extractions.method: Directly recorded from 317 neurons in human medial temporal and frontal lobes while subjects explored and navigated a virtual town.
extractions.finding: Found place-responsive cells primarily in hippocampus and landmark view-responsive cells in parahippocampal region; cells throughout frontal and temporal lobes responded to navigational goals and conjunctions of place, goal, and view.
extractions.dataset: 317 neurons recorded from human medial temporal and frontal lobes during virtual navigation
extractions.metric: Neuronal firing rate at specific spatial locations and landmark views
extractions.result: Evidence for human spatial navigation neural code with place cells and view cells

extractions.problem: Achieving practical multiple access in ultra-low latency virtual-cell-based vehicular networks without CSI feedback.
extractions.method: Applied multiuser detection (MUD) with sphere decoding for downlink interference suppression, exploiting downlink transmission characteristics for complexity reduction.
extractions.finding: Low-complexity MUD with sphere decoding achieves a favorable tradeoff between BER performance and detection complexity, enabling further reliability and lower latency in the downlink.
extractions.dataset: Simulated virtual-cell vehicular network downlink
extractions.metric: Bit error rate (BER), detection complexity, latency
extractions.baseline: Beamforming/interference alignment (unsuitable due to CSI feedback requirement)
extractions.result: Improved reliability and lower latency via sphere decoding

extractions.problem: Extracting quantitative parameters from live cell fluorescence imaging data using computational modeling.
extractions.method: Used the Virtual Cell simulation environment for analysis of fluorescence imaging data.
extractions.dataset: Live cell fluorescence imaging data

extractions.problem: Joint resource allocation combining dynamic user grouping, multi-cell cooperation, and resource block allocation for uplink virtual MIMO systems.
extractions.method: Developed dynamic multi-cell user grouping with MMSE equalization and adaptive modulation under BER constraint; proposed an efficient iterative Hungarian algorithm based on user/resource partitions.
extractions.finding: The proposed joint algorithm achieves better system throughput with BER guarantee than fixed user grouping and other schemes, with significantly reduced computational complexity.
extractions.dataset: Simulated multi-cell SC-FDMA uplink virtual MIMO system
extractions.metric: System throughput, BER, computational complexity
extractions.baseline: Fixed user grouping algorithm, other proposed schemes in literature
extractions.result: Better throughput with BER guarantee, close-to-optimal with lower complexity

extractions.problem: Developing virtual reality software tools for simulation of robotics and manufacturing cells.
Detecting and resolving resource conflicts in virtual manufacturing cells when new tasks are dynamically inserted into an initial schedule.
extractions.method: Used Bayesian network for resource conflict detection and critical chain method for conflict resolution based on virtual cell and process priority.
extractions.finding: The proposed Bayesian network detection and critical chain resolution methods are feasible and effective for handling resource conflicts from new task insertions in virtual cells.
extractions.dataset: Case study of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling
extractions.metric: Feasibility and effectiveness of conflict detection/resolution
extractions.result: Verified feasibility and effectiveness through case study

extractions.problem: Whether chemotherapy-treated cancer cells can activate CD8 T cells independent of tumor-derived MHC class I, and whether this can be exploited for immunotherapy of MHC I-deficient cancers.
extractions.method: In vitro and in vivo experiments with chemotherapy-treated cancer cells, flow cytometry for VM phenotyping, PI3K pathway analysis, humanized mouse models, and primary human CD8 T cells.
extractions.finding: Chemotherapy-treated cancer cells directly activate CD8 T cells in an MHC class I-independent manner with virtual memory phenotypes; activation requires cell-cell contact and PI3K pathway; VM CD8 T cells contribute to superior therapeutic effects on MHC I-deficient tumors.
extractions.dataset: Mouse tumor models, humanized mouse models, primary human CD8 T cells, human lymphomas
extractions.metric: VM CD8 T cell frequency, tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte analysis
extractions.result: MHC I-independent CD8 T cell activation with VM phenotype; superior effect on MHC I-deficient tumors

extractions.problem: Understanding the complex dynamical behaviors of intracellular cardiac Ca²⁺ handling, including sparks, oscillations, and traveling waves.
extractions.method: Combined experimental approaches with virtual cell computational simulation to integrate and analyze subcellular Ca²⁺ dynamics information.
extractions.finding: Intracellular Ca²⁺ handling involves coordinated interactions of numerous cellular structures producing coherent spatiotemporal patterns; virtual cell tools are essential for integrating experimental data with simulations.
extractions.dataset: Cardiac cell intracellular Ca²⁺ dynamics

extractions.problem: Training future professionals in virtual commissioning (VC) technologies for industrial robotic cells to shorten lead times and improve product quality.
extractions.method: Educational case study comparing conventional robotic cell control development against digital twin-based virtual commissioning approach.
extractions.finding: Virtual commissioning with a digital twin offers clear benefits over conventional approaches for developing and validating robotic cell control systems in educational settings.
extractions.dataset: Educational robotic cell case study
extractions.metric: Lead time, product quality (qualitative comparison)
extractions.result: VC with digital twin demonstrated benefits over conventional approach

extractions.problem: Understanding the dynamic topology of the mitochondrial inner membrane and its implications for bioenergetic function.
extractions.method: Electron tomography for structural imaging; computer modeling using the Virtual Cell program to simulate diffusion and ATP output within cristae compartments.
extractions.finding: Inner membrane is pleomorphic with narrow tubular junctions connecting cristae; transitions between morphological states involve membrane fusion/fission; Virtual Cell simulations show narrow cristae junctions restrict ADP diffusion and decrease ATP output inside cristae.
extractions.dataset: Mitochondria from various cell types including yeast
extractions.metric: ADP diffusion rates, ATP output
extractions.baseline: Textbook baffle-like fold model of inner membrane
extractions.result: Narrow cristae junctions cause ADP depletion and decreased ATP output inside cristae

extractions.problem: Moving toward computational (in silico) modeling of cellular processes as a complement to in vivo and in vitro approaches.
Achieving fault tolerant control of a proton exchange membrane fuel cell system.
extractions.method: Takagi-Sugeno virtual actuators for fault tolerant control.
extractions.dataset: Proton exchange membrane fuel cell

extractions.problem: Traditional lecture-based teaching of 3D molecular concepts in virology, cell culture, and molecular biology is limited; immersive approaches are needed to support growing biotechnology workforce demands.
extractions.method: Review of virtual reality (VR) applications for immersive, active, and performative learning in molecular life sciences, accelerated by Covid-19 pandemic shifts in education delivery.
extractions.finding: Immersive VR offers new entry points for learning abstract molecular concepts and remains relevant for face-to-face teaching; engagement of disciplinary and technical experts is key for implementation.

extractions.problem: Evaluating the predictive value of neutrophil leucocytosis for diagnosing bacterial throat infection when infectious mononucleosis prevalence is high.
extractions.method: Clinical epidemiological analysis of white cell count utility and reflection on historical antibiotic prescribing patterns.
extractions.finding: The positive predictive value of neutrophil leucocytosis for bacterial throat infection is low when infectious mononucleosis prevalence is high; historical wholesale antibiotic prescribing may have contributed to the decline of post-streptococcal diseases.
extractions.dataset: Young patient populations with sore throat
extractions.metric: Positive predictive value
extractions.result: Low predictive value when mononucleosis prevalence is high

extractions.problem: Integrating Period Batch Control (PBC) production planning with virtual manufacturing cells whose configurations change periodically.
extractions.method: Developed a model aligning PBC principles with virtual cell design goals; tested with scheduling software simulating different scheduling rules over four production weeks.
extractions.finding: PBC parameter choices (period length, stage number/contents) directly impact virtual cell configurations, machine sharing, and utilization; the model successfully integrates PBC with dynamic virtual cells.
extractions.dataset: Case study of furniture production, 4 production weeks
extractions.metric: Machine sharing, machine utilization
extractions.baseline: Classical group technology cells with PBC
extractions.result: Demonstrated PBC parameter impact on virtual cell configurations

extractions.problem: Computationally screening the vast space of organic semiconductor compounds for solar cells and light emitting diodes, balancing efficiency and accuracy.
extractions.method: Virtual screening using computational methods at varying levels of accuracy and efficiency; discussed chemical design of non-fullerene acceptors, TADF emitters, and nanographenes.
extractions.finding: Effective virtual screening for organic semiconductors rests on a delicate balance between computational efficiency and accuracy; the approach can point to new, better materials not yet synthesized.
extractions.dataset: Organic semiconductor compound libraries (non-fullerene acceptors, TADF emitters, nanographenes)
extractions.metric: Computational efficiency vs. accuracy tradeoff

extractions.problem: Finite element method encounters dispersion errors in mid-frequency acoustic problems due to its overly stiff nature.
extractions.method: Proposed a cell-based smoothed radial point interpolation method (CS-RPIM) with virtual nodes using generalized gradient smoothing for solving the Helmholtz equation.
extractions.finding: CS-RPIM with virtual nodes provides close-to-exact stiffness, leading to conspicuous decrease in dispersion errors; demonstrated high computational efficiency and insensitivity to mesh distortion.
extractions.dataset: Numerical acoustic benchmark problems (Helmholtz equation)
extractions.metric: Dispersion error, accuracy, computational efficiency, mesh distortion sensitivity
extractions.baseline: FEM-T4 (four-node tetrahedral), MIR-H8 (eight-node hexahedral with modified integration)
extractions.result: Significant improvement over FEM-T4 and MIR-H8 in dispersion error and accuracy

extractions.problem: Creating virtual groups that combine lean and agile manufacturing characteristics through enhanced production flow analysis.
Whether virtual memory T (TVM) cells can act as pathogenic effectors of inflammatory disease, specifically alopecia areata.
extractions.method: Identified a TVM cell-originated CD44super-high CD49dlo CD8+ T cell subset; investigated induction by IL-12/IL-15/IL-18 stimulation and NKG2D-dependent cytotoxicity mechanism.
extractions.finding: A TVM-originated CD44s-hi CD49dlo CD8+ T cell subset with tissue-residency features causes alopecia areata via NKG2D-dependent innate-like cytotoxicity augmented by IL-15.
extractions.dataset: Mouse models of alopecia areata
extractions.metric: NKG2D-dependent cytotoxicity, disease onset
extractions.result: TVM-originated subset identified as pathogenic driver of alopecia areata

extractions.problem: Limited frequency resources for unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) cause communication failures due to interference when numerous UAs operate at high density.
extractions.method: Proposed a multilayer virtual-cell-based TDMA resource allocation scheme that increases simultaneous UA communications by considering UA-to-ground-station distance.
extractions.finding: The multilayer virtual cell scheme increases the number of simultaneously communicating UAs and improves throughput compared to existing schemes.
extractions.dataset: Simulated unmanned aircraft system network
extractions.metric: Number of simultaneously communicating UAs, throughput
extractions.result: More efficient than existing schemes in simultaneous UA communication

extractions.problem: Tissue exhaustion and section-level mismatch limit immunohistochemistry (IHC) on cytology cell block specimens; multiplex IHC is costly.
extractions.method: Digitized cell block IHC slides with whole-slide scanner, aligned via image registration using ImageJ with custom code, recolored layers to create virtual multiplex IHC images.
extractions.finding: Virtual multiplex IHC successfully demonstrated coexpression of multiple antibodies (membranous, cytoplasmic, nuclear) without additional tissue consumption or multiplex IHC cost; mean registration accuracy was 8.40±5.52μm.
extractions.dataset: 20 cell block preparations (19 carcinomas, 1 melanoma)
extractions.metric: Mean distance between cell clusters after registration
extractions.result: Registration accuracy 8.40±5.52μm, 20/20 cases successful

extractions.problem: Defining and calculating molecular distribution functions from pair potential energies to describe thermodynamic properties of gases and liquids.
extractions.method: Derived distribution functions as power series in fugacity or inverse volume for gases; for condensed systems derived integral equations; introduced virtual cell population probabilities connecting to the cell approximation method.
extractions.finding: Established a direct connection between condensing-system theory and cell approximations for liquids; the virtual cell method permits consistent higher-order approximations for evaluating thermodynamic functions of liquids.
extractions.dataset: Theoretical molecular systems (gases and liquids)
extractions.result: Unified framework connecting condensing-system theory and cell approximations

extractions.problem: Multi-objective optimization for virtual cells as a service in cloud environments.
Predicting liquid water intrusion and transport properties in fuel cell gas diffusion layers (GDLs).
extractions.method: Developed a cluster-based full morphology (CFM) model to simulate water intrusion into dry GDL microstructure from μ-CT; compared with experimental μ-CT reconstructions at varying saturation levels.
extractions.finding: CFM simulations showed 56–95.7% agreement with μ-CT for water voxels (29–92.3% saturation); effective diffusivity predictions had max 20% error below 40% saturation; through-plane diffusivity was underpredicted by 2x.
extractions.dataset: GDL microstructure from μ-CT imaging
extractions.metric: Water voxel agreement (%), effective diffusivity error
extractions.baseline: μ-CT experimental reconstructions, literature effective diffusivity values
extractions.result: 56–95.7% voxel agreement, max 20% diffusivity error below 40% saturation

extractions.problem: Forming intelligent virtual manufacturing cells in cloud-based design and manufacturing environments.
Understanding the dynamics of topological defects (spiral wave patterns) on biological cell membranes and their parallels to quantum systems.
extractions.method: Combined direct experimental observations of starfish egg cell surfaces with mathematical continuum modeling and chemical perturbations to analyze spiral wave defect dynamics.
extractions.finding: Spiral wave defects on starfish egg cells exhibit complex braiding, pair creation, and annihilation dynamics consistent with generic continuum theory predictions, suggesting parallels between information transport in living and quantum systems.
extractions.dataset: Starfish egg cell membrane spiral wave patterns
extractions.metric: Defect braiding dynamics, pair creation/annihilation rates
extractions.result: Experimental dynamics agree with continuum theory predictions

extractions.problem: Comparing chromosomal stability of neural cells derived from different reprogramming methods (pluripotent stem cells, adult stem cells, transdifferentiation).
extractions.method: Virtual karyotyping (e-karyotyping) of over 500 neural cell samples from human and mouse sources.
extractions.finding: Neural cells from pluripotent or adult stem cells show notable genomic instability, while transdifferentiated cells are more chromosomally stable unless reprogrammed using pluripotency factors.
extractions.dataset: >500 human and mouse neural cell samples
extractions.metric: Chromosomal stability via e-karyotyping
extractions.baseline: Pluripotent stem cell-derived and adult stem cell-derived neural cells
extractions.result: Transdifferentiated cells more chromosomally stable than stem cell-derived

extractions.problem: Characterizing transmembrane potential (Vm) responses in cardiac cell strands of different curvature during uniform electric-field stimulation.
extractions.method: Optical mapping with voltage-sensitive dye on linear and U-shaped cardiac strands (100–150μm width); computational modeling of passive and active membrane current components.
extractions.finding: Curved strands exhibit field responses separable into rapid passive, slow hyperpolarizing, and delayed depolarizing components with characteristic amplitude, spatial, and temporal signatures attributable to passive behavior and active membrane currents.
extractions.dataset: Linear and U-shaped cardiac cell strands (100-150μm width)
extractions.metric: Transmembrane potential (Vm) components: amplitude, spatial and temporal signatures
extractions.result: Identified separable Vm components in curved strands with distinct signatures

extractions.problem: Clarifying whether lymphopenia-induced memory CD8 T cells and virtual memory CD8 T cells are distinct subsets or the same population.
extractions.method: Comparative review of cytokine requirements, TCR specificity roles, gene expression signatures, and immune responses of both T cell types.
extractions.finding: Lymphopenia-induced memory T cells and virtual memory T cells most likely represent a single subset, proposed to be called 'homeostatic memory T cells'.
extractions.result: Proposed unification as single subset: homeostatic memory T cells

extractions.problem: User clustering and multi-dimensional resource allocation in user-centric overlapped virtual cells of ultra-dense 5G networks to mitigate interference and improve spectrum efficiency.
extractions.method: Proposed graph-based user clustering with novel edge weight design incorporating space and power cooperation gains; used Greedy Tree Growing Algorithm (GTGA) with Gaussian-distributed threshold for dynamic cluster sizing.
extractions.finding: The proposed approach effectively reduces complexity, mitigates interference, and improves spectrum efficiency in ultra-dense networks.
extractions.dataset: Simulated ultra-dense 5G network with overlapped virtual cells
extractions.metric: Spectrum efficiency (SE), computational complexity
extractions.result: Effective interference mitigation and SE improvement

extractions.problem: Extending beyond 5G toward 6G with flexible, on-demand coverage using virtual mobile small cells to overcome fixed deployment limitations.
extractions.method: Proposed virtual mobile small cells (MSCs) created on demand using NFV and SDN, forming collaborative wireless networks with lightweight security and energy-efficient RF.
extractions.finding: MSC architecture enables reliable and efficient cell offloading with reduced protocol signaling latency, providing localized, ubiquitous, and reliable connectivity toward 6G.
extractions.dataset: Simulated mobile small cell network
extractions.metric: Cell offloading efficiency, protocol signaling latency
extractions.result: Demonstrated benefits for reliable and efficient cell offloading

extractions.problem: Sensing MMP9 activity of single suspended and adhered cancer cells.
extractions.method: Developed a virtual-droplet system for single-cell MMP9 activity detection.
extractions.dataset: Single suspended and adhered cancer cells
extractions.metric: MMP9 activity

extractions.problem: The main obstacle to broader virtual commissioning adoption is the high modeling time and effort required to create proper simulation models, which is disconnected from the project development lifecycle.
extractions.method: Proposed a semi-automatic method to transform 3D geometry of a production cell into a simulation-enabled virtual representation using VDI 2860-based behavior modeling and AutomationML, embedded in a VC workflow.
extractions.finding: The semi-automatic transformation method successfully creates high-fidelity simulation models from 3D geometry combined with intuitive behavior descriptions; demonstrated on a conveyor production cell using multiple commercial tools.
extractions.dataset: Production cell with conveyors (case study)
extractions.metric: Modeling time and effort, simulation fidelity
extractions.result: Semi-automatic method demonstrated on conveyor production cell
